title: nie-tui slash command dispatcher: core commands
description:
**What:** Implement handle_slash() — dispatches /command input to the appropriate action. Core commands: /iam, /who, /me, /alias, /dm, /clear, /help, /quit, /!, /cat.

**Algorithm:**
1. In tui/src/event.rs (or new tui/src/slash.rs), implement async fn handle_slash(state: &mut AppState, tx: &mpsc::Sender<JsonRpcRequest>, line: &str) -> anyhow::Result<()>:
2. Split on first space: let (cmd, rest) = line[1..].split_once(' ').unwrap_or((&line[1..], ""));
3. Match cmd (case-insensitive):
   - "quit" | "q": state.quit = true.
   - "help" | "h": push ChatLine::System(HELP_TEXT.to_string()).
   - "clear": state.messages.clear(); state.scroll_offset = 0.
   - "who": for each user in state.online, push ChatLine::System(format!("  {} {}", if i==0 {"[A]"} else {"   "}, state.display_name(pub_id))).
   - "iam" where !rest.is_empty(): send SetNicknameParams { nickname: rest.trim().to_string() } via JsonRpcRequest. Update state.own_profile["name"] = nickname.
   - "alias" where rest has two tokens: parse "name pubid", store in state.local_names[pubid]=name, persist via Contacts::add.
   - "me" where !rest.is_empty(): send as ClearMessage::Chat { text: format!("\x01ACTION {}\x01", rest) }.
   - "dm" where rest has peer and message: parse "handle msg"; resolve handle to pub_id via display_name inverse; send WhisperParams { to: pub_id, payload }.
   - "!" where !rest.is_empty(): shlex::split(rest) → argv; if argv is empty, push system error; else Command::new(&argv[0]).args(&argv[1..]).output(); if output.stdout.len() <= 4096, send as chat; else truncate. NEVER use sh -c. ANSI-escape the output before displaying.
   - "cat" where !rest.is_empty(): read file with fs::read_to_string (max 4096 bytes); send as chat message.
   - "set" where rest has key and value: state.own_profile[key] = value; broadcast profile update as ClearMessage::Profile.
   - "unset" where !rest.is_empty(): remove state.own_profile[key]; broadcast profile update.
   - "profile" [handle]: display own or peer's profile as system messages.
   - unknown: push ChatLine::System(format!("Unknown command: /{cmd}. Type /help.")).

4. HELP_TEXT constant lists all slash commands with one-line descriptions.

**Defensive programming rules:**
- /! MUST use shlex::split then Command::new NOT sh -c. This is a security invariant (see CLAUDE.md §/! Shell Injection Boundary). Comment this explicitly.
- /cat: cap at 4096 bytes; if file not found, push system error (not panic).
- /dm: if handle not in contacts or online, push system error with available handles.
- /iam nickname: validate non-empty after trim; max 64 chars.
- /! output: apply ANSI escape stripping before display and before sending to chat room (peers' terminals are protected too).

**Agent team:** Standard (R+I+V). R reads cli/src/commands.rs handle_slash (around line 2931) for the pattern. I reimplements for TUI (same semantics, different state struct). V runs tests.

**Tests:**
- Test /who with 3 online users verifies first has [A] prefix.
- Test /iam empty arg pushes system error.
- Test /! with shell metacharacters (semicolon, pipe) passes literal args (not interpreted by shell).
- Test /cat nonexistent file pushes system error without panic.
- Test /clear empties messages and resets scroll_offset.
- Test unknown command pushes system error.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p nie-tui slash_tests all pass; /! semicolon test confirms no shell injection; cargo clippy -p nie-tui exits 0
